title: John Romita Sr.
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Otheruses|John}} {{Person |image=[[File:Johnromitasr.jpg]] |name=John Romita Sr. |birth=[[January 24]], [[1930s#1930|1930]]
[[Wikipedia:Brooklyn|Brooklyn]], [[Wikipedia:New York City|New York City]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/06/14/arts/john-romita-sr-dead.html|text=John Romita Sr., Creative Force at Marvel Comics, Is Dead at 93|author=Derrick Bryson Taylor ; George Gene Gustines|date=2023-06-14|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|''The New York Times'']]|archivedate=20230614104727}} |death=[[June 12]], [[2023]] |listedpronouns= |nationality= |occupation=*Penciller *Inker *Cover artist |companies= |website= }} '''John Romita Sr.''' ([[January 24]], [[1930s#1930|1930]] – [[June 12]], [[2023]]) was the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] penciller and cover artist for several issues of [[Marvel Comics]]' [[1986]] [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] series: [[Droids (1986) 1|''Droids'' 1]], [[Droids (1986) 2|''Droids'' 2]], and [[Droids (1986) 3|''Droids'' 3]]. Romita also worked on [[Droids (1986) 4|''Droids'' 4]] and [[Droids (1986) 5|''Droids'' 5]]. He created [[Kugg]], the [[Ranger X-1]] craft and the [[Destroyer (droid)|Destroyer]]. In the [[1950s]], Romita worked for [[Stan Lee]] at [[Wikipedia:Atlas Comics (1950s)|Atlas Comics]], the precursor to Marvel, on such titles as ''[[Wikipedia:Captain America Comics|Captain America]]''. A brief run at [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC]] followed, and in [[1960s#1965|1965]] he reunited with Lee under the new Marvel banner. There, he famously worked on ''[[Wikipedia:The Amazing Spider-Man|The Amazing Spider-Man]]'' and designed the character of [[Wikipedia:Mary Jane Watson|Mary Jane Watson]]. He later served as Marvel's Art Director.{{WebCite|url=https://www.tcj.com/john-romita-1930-2023/|text=John Romita, 1930-2023|work=The Comics Journal|author=Steven Ringgenberg|date=2023-06-17|archivedate=20250723070652}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images by John Romita}} {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{WP|John Romita Sr.}} [[Category:Comic book cover artists]] [[Category:Comic book pencillers]]
